title: myosotis palustris var. laxa
intro: Myosotis palustris var. laxa, also known as the marsh forget-me-not, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and promoting digestion. Herbal preparations of marsh forget-me-not typically involve infusions, tinctures, or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams and ointments.
